title: elaphoglossum humbertii
intro: Elaphoglossum humbertii, also known as Humbert's Elaphoglossum, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae and is native to Madagascar. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Elaphoglossum humbertii include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a treatment for skin conditions and wounds. In terms of herbal preparations, the leaves of the Elaphoglossum humbertii plant can be used to make a tea or infusion, which is typically brewed in hot water to release its medicinal properties.
